title: Commodities Brief — 12 May 2026
intro:
COMMODITIES BRIEF
12 May 2026 • 06:11 CET

Headline: Commodities Markets — Session Overview

Commodities rally continues amid weakening dollar and mounting supercycle evidence. Risk-on sentiment supports industrial metals while agricultural complex gains on supply concerns. Real rates remain supportive for precious metals complex.

Precious Metals
Gold consolidates at $4717.20/oz (-0.03% intraday)

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
COMMODITIES BRIEF
12 May 2026 • 06:11 CET

Headline: Commodities Markets — Session Overview

Commodities rally continues amid weakening dollar and mounting supercycle evidence. Risk-on sentiment supports industrial metals while agricultural complex gains on supply concerns. Real rates remain supportive for precious metals complex.

Precious Metals
Gold consolidates at $4717.20/oz (-0.03% intraday), finding support from negative real yields and central bank diversification flows. Silver outperforms at $85.96/oz (+0.55%), benefiting from industrial demand recovery. Platinum weakens to $2097.80/oz (-

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
0.84%) on automotive sector headwinds, while palladium retreats to $1503.50/oz amid supply normalization. Copper surges to $6.49/lb (+1.23%), confirming global growth acceleration thesis.

Energy
Oil complex extends gains with WTI at $99.40/bbl (+1.36%) and Brent at $105.29/bbl (+1.04%) on tightening fundamentals. OPEC+ production discipline and geopolitical premium support higher prices. Brent-WTI spread widens to $5.89/bbl reflecting refining capacity constraints. Natural gas climbs to $2.93/MMBtu (+0.69%) on storage deficit concerns.

Agriculture
Grains rally sharply with wheat leading at $641.

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
50/bu (+3.09%) on European drought concerns. Corn advances to $474.50/bu (+2.98%) while soybeans reach $1214.25/bu (+1.21%) as La Nina weather patterns threaten South American crops.

Trade Ideas
LONG: Silver (industrial demand recovery), Wheat (weather premium), Copper (supercycle acceleration)
SHORT/HEDGE: Palladium (oversupply), Long-dated WTI puts (demand destruction risk)

Key Risks
Federal Reserve pivot toward aggressive tightening could reverse dollar weakness. Chinese property sector deterioration threatens industrial metals demand. Escalating Middle East tensions present upside energy risk.
